The Owner

Meet the Owner

Built locally. Run with care.

Three years ago I started knocking on doors in Columbia with a bucket, a squeegee, and a goal: to do honest, careful work for the homes in my community.

"Today Clear View serves homeowners across Eastern Connecticut with a trained team and professional equipment — but the standard is exactly the same. Meticulous work. Honest pricing. Real local accountability."

I still personally visit every property for the initial estimate. When you hire Clear View, you're hiring people who live where they work — and people who'll be back next year, too.

How We Clean

Pure water. Spotless results.

We use professional water-fed pole technology — the gold standard in modern window cleaning — paired with careful hand-work on the inside.

Professional Equipment

The Water-Fed Pole System

We use 100% deionized purified water pushed through a soft-bristle brush on a telescoping pole. No detergents. No ladders leaning on your siding. No streaks left behind.

✓
Safer for your home — we clean from the ground, no ladders pressed against your gutters or siding.
✓
Zero chemicals — safe for plants, pets, kids, and our environment.
✓
Stays cleaner longer — pure water leaves no detergent residue, so dust and pollen don't stick.
✓
Reaches every window — second-story, dormers, picture windows — all from solid ground.
Our 4-Step Process

Simple, careful, done right.

01

Free Estimate

We visit your home, walk the property with you, and provide an upfront written quote. No pressure, no surprises.

02

Interior Hand-Wash

We hand-clean every inside pane with a streak-free solution. Sills and tracks are detailed. Furniture is protected.

03

Exterior Pure Water

Our water-fed pole system uses purified water to scrub and rinse every exterior window — frame to frame.

04

Final Walk-Through

We walk through with you before we leave. Anything missed? We fix it on the spot. That's the guarantee.

What We Offer

Focused on what we do best.

Residential window cleaning, done properly — with the equipment, training, and care your home deserves.

Window Cleaning

Interior and exterior windows hand-cleaned and pure-water rinsed. Streak-free, every time.

Screen Cleaning

Pulled, deep-washed off-frame, dried, and reinstalled. Airflow restored, appearance like new.

Sill & Track Detailing

Built-up dirt, dead bugs, and grime carefully cleaned out of sills and tracks. Often the biggest visible difference.

Hard Water Stain Removal

Mineral stains from sprinklers and rain runoff that regular cleaning can't touch. We restore glass clarity.

Common Questions

Everything you might want to know before booking.

Quick answers to the questions we hear most often. Don't see yours? Call (860) 966-3149 or use the quote form.

How much does window cleaning cost in Connecticut?
Most residential jobs in Eastern CT fall between $250 and $700 depending on square footage, number of stories, how dirty the windows are, and any add-ons like screens or solar panels. Use the instant estimator above to see a ballpark in under 60 seconds. The final price is always confirmed at a free in-person walkthrough — no surprises.
Do you fix broken or foggy windows?
We don't replace glass ourselves — we partner with Columbia Glass, a family-owned Eastern CT shop that's been making custom insulated glass units in-house since 1953. They handle broken panes, fogged seals, tempered glass, trapezoids, replacement windows, and entry doors. Call us at (860) 966-3149 and we'll coordinate the work alongside your cleaning.
How often should I get my windows cleaned?
For most homes in Eastern CT we recommend twice a year — once in spring (to clear pollen, winter salt, and tree debris) and once in fall (to remove summer dust and prepare for the cold months). Homes near busy roads, construction, or in heavily wooded areas often benefit from quarterly cleanings.
Do I need to be home during the cleaning?
Not at all. For exterior-only jobs you don't need to be home — we'll text you photos when we're done. For interior cleanings someone needs to let us in, but you're welcome to leave once we get started. We're fully insured and treat every home with the same respect we'd want for our own.
What if it rains the day of my appointment?
Light rain actually doesn't affect a properly cleaned window — the soap and squeegee technique we use leaves a clean surface, and clean glass repels water cleanly. For heavy rain or storms we'll reach out the day before to reschedule at no charge. Your time matters as much as ours.
What's the water-fed pole system you use?
It's a professional method for cleaning high windows safely from the ground. Tap water runs through a filtration system that strips out all minerals, leaving 100% pure water. That pure water cleans the glass and dries spot-free with no soap, no chemicals, and no ladders on the second story. It's safer for your home, your landscaping, and our team — and the result is a streak-free finish even on three-story homes and cathedral windows.
Are you insured?
Yes — we carry $1,000,000 in general liability insurance through Hiscox. Certificate of insurance is available on request before your appointment. While we treat your home carefully and accidents are extremely rare, the coverage protects you in the unlikely event something goes wrong.
What towns do you serve?
We're based in Columbia, CT and regularly serve Coventry, Mansfield, Storrs, Andover, Tolland, Manchester, Glastonbury, Vernon, Bolton, and the surrounding Eastern Connecticut area. If your town isn't listed, call us — we often travel a bit further for larger jobs or repeat customers.
Can you remove hard water stains and mineral spots?
Yes. Hard water stains, sprinkler spots, and mineral buildup require a separate treatment using specialized restoration products. We typically charge $120 for hard water stain removal as an add-on. Severe cases (years of buildup) may need a free in-person assessment to confirm pricing. Standard window cleaning alone won't remove these — they need the dedicated treatment.
Do you clean screens, sills, and tracks?
Screens are an optional add-on at $4 each — we remove, wash, and reinstall every screen. Sills and tracks are detailed as part of every interior cleaning at no extra charge. We don't believe in nickel-and-diming on the basics.
How long does a typical job take?
Most homes take 2–4 hours depending on size and service type. A 2,000 sqft home with interior and exterior plus screens usually runs about 3 hours. We'll give you an exact time estimate during the walkthrough so you can plan your day.
Your Appointment

How to prepare for your cleaning.

A smooth job starts with a few quick steps. Here's everything you need to know before we arrive.

1

Free walkthrough first

After you book, we schedule a free in-person walkthrough to count windows, identify add-ons, and lock in your final price. No surprises later.

2

Clear sills & tracks

Remove items from window sills and tracks before we arrive — picture frames, plants, candles. This lets us work faster and protects your belongings.

3

Lock up pets

For interior cleanings, please secure cats and dogs in a separate room. We love pets but they get curious about ladders and squeegees.

4

We bring everything

Water, ladders, squeegees, water-fed pole system, and our own outdoor faucet adapter. You don't need to prep anything else.

5

You don't have to be home

For exterior-only jobs you can be anywhere — we send completion photos. For interior, just let us in and feel free to go about your day.

6

Payment after completion

We collect payment only after the job is done and you're happy with the result. Cash, check, Venmo, Zelle, or card — whichever works for you.
